prompts: Mitosis consists of several stages: prophase, metaphase, anaphase, and telophase. In prophase, chromosomes condense and become visible, and spindle fibers begin to form. During metaphase, the chromosomes align at the cell equator. Anaphase follows, where sister chromatids are separated and pulled toward opposite poles. In telophase, nuclear membranes reform around the separated chromosome sets. Cytokinesis, the division of the cytoplasm, occurs after mitosis, resulting in two separate daughter cells. Together, these stages ensure accurate distribution of chromosomes to daughter cells.
